ddls:
CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manager of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department (e.g., city, country) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "positions" (
  "position_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the position */,
  "position_name" TEXT /* Name of the position */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the position */,
  "grade" INTEGER /* Grade or level of the position */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the position */,
  "is_management_position" INTEGER /* Indicates if the position is a management position */,
  "required_education" TEXT /* Required education level for the position */,
  "required_experience" INTEGER /* Required years of experience for the position */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("position_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nationalities" (
  "nationality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the nationality */,
  "nationality_name" TEXT /* Name of the nationality */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the nationality */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO country code for the nationality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("nationality_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the company */,
  "parent_company_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent company (if applicable) */,
  "ceo_name" TEXT /* Name of the CEO of the company */,
  "headquarters_location" TEXT /* Location of the company's headquarters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_parent_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hr_managers" (
  "hr_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the HR manager */,
  "hr_name" TEXT /* Name of the HR manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the HR manager */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the HR manager */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the HR manager is responsible for */,
  "is_hr_director" INTEGER /* Indicates if the HR manager is an HR director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hr_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hr_managers_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO country code for the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)
column_names: Reference to the manager of the department

Grade or level of the position
